data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Mumgaard, Whyte, Greenwald, Hartwig, Brunner, and Sorbom, has raised $3.0B to date against a stated valuation exceeding $5.0B. The valuation analysis relies on two applicable triangulation methods against a comp universe of nine private peers and one public comparable (LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector.

Stage-Progression methodology applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the last post-money valuation of $5.0B, yielding $9.5B. Peer-Median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage companies—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—produces a median of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $500M.

The two-method triangulation supports a median valuation of $4.9B against the stated $5.0B. The implied range is $264M (low) to $10.9B (high), reflecting material tension between stage-based progression and peer multiples. The wide dispersion between peer-median ($310M) and stage-progression ($9.5B) suggests either that Commonwealth occupies a materially differentiated risk-return profile relative to the peer set, or that the current valuation embeds significant early-stage optionality premiums not reflected in comparable transactions. Capital intensity, technology maturity, and pathway to commercialization remain key drivers of valuation uncertainty.
